title: lathyrus pannonicus subsp. pannonicus
intro: Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. pannonicus, also known as the Hungarian vetch or Pannonic pea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat urinary tract issues. The health benefits of Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. pannonicus include its potential to reduce blood pressure, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. pannonicus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the plant's seeds, leaves, or roots.
